turns.user_message: We need a solution for senior leadership.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Happy to help narrow that down. Who is this meant for?

turns.user_message: The pool consists of CXOs, director-level postions; people with more than 15 years of experience.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For such roles, the OPQ32r is the right instrument — it measures 32 workplace behaviour dimensions including strategic thinking, influencing style, and leadership. One question before I commit to a report format:is this for a newly created position, or developmental feedback for an executive already in role?

turns.user_message: Selection — comparing candidates against a leadership benchmark.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For selection with a leadership benchmark, the instrument plus two relevant report formats:

turns.user_message: Perfect, that's what we need.
turns.expected_agent_reply: The OPQ32r is what your candidates complete — the UCF and Leadership Reports are the outputs you receive, both runnable from a single administration.

turns.user_message: I'm hiring a senior Rust engineer for high-performance networking infrastructure. What assessments should I use?
turns.expected_agent_reply: SHL's catalog doesn't currently include a Rust-specific knowledge test. The closest fit for a senior IC is Smart Interview Live Coding — an adaptive live-coding interview where your panel can frame Rust-specific tasks directly. Linux Programming covers systems depth, and Networking and Implementation covers the infrastructure dimension. Want me to build a shortlist from these?

turns.user_message: Yes, go ahead. Should I also add a cognitive test for this level?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Yes — Verify G+ is appropriate for senior technical candidates, covering inductive, numerical, and deductive reasoning in a single adaptive test. Full shortlist: I'm including OPQ32r by default as the personality component for a senior IC hire — say the word if you'd rather drop it.

turns.user_message: That works. Thanks.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Note there's no Rust-specific test in the catalog — worth revisiting if that changes.

turns.user_message: We're screening 500 entry-level contact centre agents. Inbound calls, customer service focus. What should we use?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Before I shape the stack — what language are the calls in? That drives which spoken-language screen we use.

turns.user_message: English.
turns.expected_agent_reply: SVAR has four English variants in the catalog: US, UK, Australian, and Indian accent. The choice matters because the screen is calibrated for the accent your callers will hear. Which fits your operation?

turns.user_message: US.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For high-volume entry-level contact centre screening in English US, the right stack layers a spoken language screen, a simulation, and a behavioural fit component:
expected_recommendations: Entry Level Customer Serv - Retail & Contact Center

turns.user_message: Is the Contact Center Call Simulation different from the Customer Service Phone Simulation?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Yes — distinct products. The Customer Service Phone Simulation is an older bundled solution (B, P, S) combining personality, behaviour, and simulation in one package. The Contact Center Call Simulation (New) is a standalone, newer simulation focused purely on the in-call interaction. Many clients use the new simulation for volume screening and the older solution for finalist-stage depth.

turns.user_message: Perfect — new simulation for volume, old solution for finalists. Confirmed.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Good two-stage design.
expected_recommendations: Entry Level Customer Serv - Retail & Contact Center

turns.user_message: Hiring graduate financial analysts — final-year students, no work experience. We need numerical reasoning and a finance knowledge test.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For graduate-level financial analysts:

turns.user_message: Good. Can you also add a situational judgement element — work-context decision making for graduates?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Added Graduate Scenarios — SHL's situational judgement test designed specifically for graduate-level candidates. The previous three items are unchanged:

turns.user_message: That covers it. Numerical + Graduate Scenarios as first filter, domain tests for shortlisted candidates.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Good two-stage design. That keeps the initial screen fast while reserving knowledge tests for candidates who've cleared the cognitive bar.

turns.user_message: As part of our restructuring and annual talent audit, we need to re-skill our Sales organization. What solutions do you recommend?
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a compact audit-and-development stack: **self-reported skills** (GSA plus its development report for re-skilling initiative), **personality** via OPQ32r, a **sales-specific OPQ view** with optional motivators, and **Sales Transformation** for how reps show up in a digital-first selling model. First-line sales managers can follow the same spine and use the Sales Manager Sales Transformation report from the catalog when you split leader vs. rep reporting.

turns.user_message: What’s the difference between OPQ and OPQ MQ Sales Report?
turns.expected_agent_reply: **OPQ (OPQ32r)** is the underlying **personality questionnaire**: a broad, standard measure of workplace behavioural style used across roles and decisions (development, team fit, etc.). **OPQ MQ Sales Report** is a **reporting product**, not a different questionnaire. It summarizes OPQ results in a **sales-specific** way—graphical and narrative emphasis on behaviours tied to sales success. You can **optionally** add the **Motivation Questionnaire (MQ)** so the same report also reflects **sales motivators and drives**; without MQ, you still get the sales-framed OPQ story. So: one assessment instrument (OPQ32r) for personality; the Sales Report is how you **read** those results for sellers (and optionally enrich with MQ).

turns.user_message: Clear. We’ll use OPQ for everyone and add MQ only where we want motivators in the Sales Report; keeping the five solutions as our audit stack.
turns.expected_agent_reply: That matches how the catalog is built: OPQ32r once, OPQ MQ Sales Report for sales-language feedback and optional MQ, plus GSA and its development report for skills and associated re-skilling, and Sales Transformation 2.0 for rep-level digital selling behaviours.

turns.user_message: We're hiring plant operators for a chemical facility. Safety is absolute top priority — reliability, procedure compliance, never cutting corners. What do you recommend?
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a safety-critical frontline role where dependability and rule compliance are the primary concern, the assessment focus must be on personality predictors of safety behaviour — not just knowledge tests. A knowledge test tells you what someone knows about safety; a personality instrument predicts whether they'll actually follow through.

turns.user_message: What's the difference between the DSI and the Safety & Dependability 8.0?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Both measure safety-relevant personality, but at different levels. The DSI is a standalone instrument measuring integrity, reliability, and safety attitudes — used across sectors. The Manufacturing & Industrial Safety & Dependability 8.0 is a sector-specific bundled solution with norms calibrated to manufacturing and industrial workforces. If your facility is industrial-classified, the 8.0 gives you industry norms. If you want a general standalone instrument, the DSI is the right choice.

turns.user_message: We're industrial. The 8.0 bundle is the right fit. Confirmed.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Good choice for an industrial context. Shortlist confirmed.

turns.user_message: We're hiring bilingual healthcare admin staff in South Texas — they handle patient records and need to be assessed in Spanish. HIPAA compliance is critical. What assessments work?
turns.expected_agent_reply: There's a real catalog constraint here: the role-specific knowledge tests for healthcare admin — HIPAA (Security), Medical Terminology, Microsoft Word — are English-only. The personality measures (OPQ32r, DSI) do support Latin American Spanish. Two ways to run this: (a) Hybrid: knowledge tests in English, personality in Spanish. Workable if your candidates are functionally bilingual at a working level for the English knowledge tests. (b) Personality-only in Spanish, and assess HIPAA/medical terminology through your own structured interview. Trades off measurement rigour for language fit. Which fits your candidate pool better?

turns.user_message: They're functionally bilingual — English fluent for written work. Go with the hybrid.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a trust-sensitive role with patient-record access, two complementary personality measures: DSI focuses specifically on dependability and safety-critical reliability — directly relevant to HIPAA-protected information handling. OPQ32r gives a broader workplace-behaviour profile. Both run in Latin American Spanish. Full hybrid battery:

turns.user_message: Are we legally required under HIPAA to test all staff who touch patient records? And does this SHL test satisfy that requirement?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Those are legal compliance questions outside what I can advise on — I can help you select assessments, but not interpret regulatory obligations or whether a specific test satisfies a legal requirement. Your legal or compliance team is the right resource for that. What I can confirm: HIPAA (Security) measures knowledge of HIPAA security provisions. Whether using it fulfils a particular regulatory obligation is a question for counsel.

turns.user_message: Understood. Keep the shortlist as-is.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Confirmed. Hybrid battery as above — knowledge tests in English, DSI and OPQ32r in Latin American Spanish.

turns.user_message: I need to quickly screen admin assistants for Excel and Word daily.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a quick knowledge check, the knowledge-only variants are the right fit — they test conceptual understanding. We also have end-to-end simulations to test these capabilties but I have excluded them due to the time constraint. I've also included OPQ32r as a default personality measure — admin-assistant hires benefit from a behavioural fit signal beyond pure tool knowledge. Say the word if you'd prefer to skip personality.

turns.user_message: In that case, I am OK with adding a simulation - we want to capture the capabilties.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Understood.Here's the updated list:

turns.user_message: That's good.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Confirmed.

turns.user_message: Here's the JD for an engineer we need to fill. Can you recommend an assessment battery?
turns.expected_agent_reply: That JD spans seven distinct areas — Core Java, Spring, REST APIs, Angular, SQL, AWS, and Docker. The catalog has dedicated knowledge tests for each. A focused recommendation needs to know what the candidate will actually own. To narrow this down: Is this a backend-leaning role (Java / Spring / SQL heavy) or a frontend-heavy role or a true balanced full-stack role with significant Angular work?

turns.user_message: Backend-leaning. Day-one priorities are Core Java and Spring; SQL is constant. Angular is occasional — they'd review frontend PRs but not own features.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Understood — Java, Spring, and SQL as primary; Angular as secondary review-only. One more question before I shape the battery: is the seniority closer to a senior IC (deep backend ownership, leads design on their service) or a tech lead (sets architecture across services, reviews other engineers' work)? That changes whether we lean on a knowledge-heavy battery or add a leadership/scenarios layer.

turns.user_message: Senior IC. They lead design on their own services but don't manage other engineers directly.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a senior IC backend engineer with Java / Spring / SQL primary and Angular secondary, here's a first shortlist focused on what they'll actually own:

turns.user_message: Add AWS and Docker. Drop REST — the API design signal will already come through in Spring and the live interview.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Updated — REST out, AWS and Docker in:

turns.user_message: On Java — they'd be working on existing services, not greenfield. Is the Advanced level the right pick?
turns.expected_agent_reply: Yes. Advanced covers concurrency, JVM internals, performance tuning, and the kind of design-level Java that comes up when extending production microservices — which matches "owns end-to-end microservice delivery". The Entry-Level test is calibrated for graduate hires reading and modifying existing code, which undershoots a senior IC. Keeping Advanced.

turns.user_message: Do we really need Verify G+ on top of all the technical tests? Feels redundant.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Not redundant — they measure different things. The technical tests confirm the candidate already knows the stack. Verify G+ measures general reasoning ability, which predicts how quickly they'll learn parts of the stack they haven't seen and adapt when production throws something unfamiliar at them. For a senior IC making architectural calls, that signal matters. That said, if you want a leaner shortlist, dropping Verify G+ is a valid tradeoff and the battery still stands. Your call.

turns.user_message: Keep Verify G+. Locking it in.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Final battery — Java Advanced, Spring, SQL, AWS, and Docker as the technical core; Verify G+ for reasoning; OPQ32r for personality fit on a senior IC who'll mentor and contribute to architecture.

turns.user_message: We run a graduate management trainee scheme. We need a full battery — cognitive, personality, and situational judgement. All recent graduates.
turns.expected_agent_reply: For a graduate management trainee battery covering all three dimensions:

turns.user_message: But can you remove the OPQ32r and replace it with something shorter? Candidates complain it takes too long.
turns.expected_agent_reply: OPQ32r is the most relevant solution for your need. As such, there is no shorter alternative to be used as its replacement.

turns.user_message: Drop the OPQ. Final list: Verify G+ and Graduate Scenarios.
turns.expected_agent_reply: Updated. OPQ32r removed. Final shortlist confirmed.
